title: Brachial Plexus Schematic
structures: medial cutaneous nerve of the arm
medial cutaneous nerve of the forearm
description: A structural schematic diagram mapping the brachial plexus from its origin at the C5-T1 spinal roots through its organization into trunks, divisions, and cords, terminating in the five major peripheral nerves of the upper limb (musculocutaneous, axillary, radial, median, and ulnar nerves), along with smaller collateral branches.
clinical_notes: Upper trunk injuries (C5-C6) can result in Erb's palsy ('waiter's tip' posture) affecting shoulder abduction and elbow flexion. Lower trunk injuries (C8-T1) can result in Klumpke's palsy, causing a claw hand deformity. OT role: parent education on handling (for obstetric injuries), fabrication of resting or functional splints to prevent contractures, and therapeutic exercises to maintain PROM and encourage active movement as reinnervation occurs.
